pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i wake up and for a split second i forget... and then the silence hits me all over again. it's like i'm just walking through my own house as a ghost. there's no point to any of it. i'm just going through the motions of a life that doesn't even belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: i was looking at the stars tonight and just felt... nothing. like, everything we do, every single thing we care about, it's just a tiny blip in a cold, dead universe. in a billion years none of this matters. why even bother trying to keep going when the end result is just total nothingness anyway?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm even logging on. everything just feels... heavy. like, i spent six years on this research and now the lab is losing funding and my advisor won't even look me in the eye. it feels like my whole life was built on a foundation of nothing. i'm just so tired of fighting for something that doesn't even want me back.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone, and with him, the last shred of reason I held dear. Every shadow in this wretched room seems to whisper of my inadequacy, of a life lived only to be discarded like a wilted rose. I find myself longing for the quiet dark... to simply dissolve into the mist and never wake to this hollow ache again. Is there no mercy in this cruel world but the silence of the grave?
